text.title: devops-lb-algorithms Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for devops-lb-algorithms as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using IP Hash for general web traffic as a substitute for proper session management.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: IP Hash breaks when clients share a NAT gateway (all traffic hits one backend) or use mobile networks (IP changes break session). Use server-side session storage (Redis) or cookie-based affinity instead.

failure-mode.description: Choosing L4 for microservices to "avoid overhead".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Microservices require path-based routing, header injection (trace IDs, auth), and SSL termination — none of which L4 provides. The overhead of L7 HTTP parsing is negligible compared to the routing flexibility gained.
